title: You're ready, but know you can make mistakes — title-as-permission-slip
html:
TL;DR

  • Empty-body Apple Note from August 26, 2019 — title as permission slip: "You're ready, but know you can make mistakes." Both halves are doing work.
  • Same calendar day as the dream of dad's key note — paired output: subconscious-symbol-stack in the morning, conscious-self-talk in the same Apple Notes session. Both filed a few days after the dad died note.
  • The grammar is the disconnected from outcome frame in miniature — go (you're ready) AND don't grip (mistakes are allowed). Permission and push, one sentence.
  • Anchors the late-August-2019 teen era cluster of self-talk notes during the immediate-aftermath of the dad-grief reset — the week Yahir is rebuilding operating instructions from scratch.
  • Maps directly to the "I have nothing to lose, thus be brave enough to try" line that closes the three things manifest two years later — same dual-clause permission-and-push form.

Note

Eleven words. Yahir at 16, four days after writing "my dad dead Augusta 24th", issuing himself the dual-clause line a coach would give a player before they go in. You're ready is the push; know you can make mistakes is the cushion that makes the push survivable. Together they pre-emptively defang the fear of not doing AND the fear of doing and failing in one breath.

That this note exists on the same day as the dad's key dream is not coincidence — the conscious mind is doing the daylight version of the dream's nighttime work: am I authorized to act now. The letter to self is Yahir's authorization, written and saved.

Connections

  • 2019 08 26 dream of dads key — same-day dream-log
  • 2019 08 25 my dad dead augusta 24th — four days prior, the rupture
  • — the form
  • disconnected from outcome — the frame
  • 2021 10 26 three things manifest — the same dual-clause form, two years later
  • fear of not doing — what the line defangs
